speaker: VIOLA
text: Save thee, friend, and thy music. Dost thou live

speaker: VIOLA
text: by thy tabor?

speaker: FOOL
text: No, sir, I live by the church.

speaker: VIOLA
text: Art thou a churchman?

speaker: FOOL
text: No such matter, sir. I do live by the church, for I

speaker: FOOL
text: do live at my house, and my house doth stand by the

speaker: FOOL
text: church.

speaker: VIOLA
text: So thou mayst say the king lies by a beggar if a

speaker: VIOLA
text: beggar dwell near him, or the church stands by thy

speaker: VIOLA
text: tabor if thy tabor stand by the church.

speaker: FOOL
text: You have said, sir. To see this age! A sentence is

speaker: FOOL
text: but a chev’ril glove to a good wit. How quickly the

speaker: FOOL
text: wrong side may be turned outward!

speaker: VIOLA
text: Nay, that’s certain. They that dally nicely with

speaker: VIOLA
text: words may quickly make them wanton.

speaker: FOOL
text: I would therefore my sister had had no name,

speaker: FOOL
text: sir.

speaker: VIOLA
text: Why, man?

speaker: FOOL
text: Why, sir, her name’s a word, and to dally with

speaker: FOOL
text: that word might make my sister wanton. But,

speaker: FOOL
text: indeed, words are very rascals since bonds disgraced

speaker: FOOL
text: them.

speaker: VIOLA
text: Thy reason, man?

speaker: FOOL
text: Troth, sir, I can yield you none without words,

speaker: FOOL
text: and words are grown so false I am loath to prove

speaker: FOOL
text: reason with them.

speaker: VIOLA
text: I warrant thou art a merry fellow and car’st for

speaker: VIOLA
text: nothing.

speaker: FOOL
text: Not so, sir. I do care for something. But in my

speaker: FOOL
text: conscience, sir, I do not care for you. If that be to

speaker: FOOL
text: care for nothing, sir, I would it would make you

speaker: FOOL
text: invisible.

speaker: VIOLA
text: Art not thou the Lady Olivia’s Fool?

speaker: FOOL
text: No, indeed, sir. The Lady Olivia has no folly. She

speaker: FOOL
text: will keep no Fool, sir, till she be married, and Fools

speaker: FOOL
text: are as like husbands as pilchers are to herrings: the

speaker: FOOL
text: husband’s the bigger. I am indeed not her Fool but

speaker: FOOL
text: her corrupter of words.

speaker: VIOLA
text: I saw thee late at the Count Orsino’s.

speaker: FOOL
text: Foolery, sir, does walk about the orb like the

speaker: FOOL
text: sun; it shines everywhere. I would be sorry, sir, but

speaker: FOOL
text: the Fool should be as oft with your master as with

speaker: FOOL
text: my mistress. I think I saw your Wisdom there.

speaker: VIOLA
text: Nay, an thou pass upon me, I’ll no more with

speaker: VIOLA
text: thee. Hold, there’s expenses for thee.

speaker: FOOL
text: Now Jove, in his next commodity of hair, send

speaker: FOOL
text: thee a beard!

speaker: VIOLA
text: By my troth I’ll tell thee, I am almost sick for

speaker: VIOLA
text:
one,
                 
                aside
               though I would not have it grow on my

speaker: VIOLA
text: chin.—Is thy lady within?

speaker: FOOL
text: Would not a pair of these have bred, sir?

speaker: VIOLA
text: Yes, being kept together and put to use.

speaker: FOOL
text: I would play Lord Pandarus of Phrygia, sir, to

speaker: FOOL
text: bring a Cressida to this Troilus.

speaker: VIOLA
text: I understand you, sir. ’Tis well begged.

speaker: FOOL
text: The matter I hope is not great, sir, begging but a

speaker: FOOL
text: beggar: Cressida was a beggar. My lady is within, sir.

speaker: FOOL
text: I will conster to them whence you come. Who you

speaker: FOOL
text: are and what you would are out of my welkin—I

speaker: FOOL
text: might say “element,” but the word is overworn.

speaker: VIOLA
text: This fellow is wise enough to play the Fool,

speaker: VIOLA
text: And to do that well craves a kind of wit.

speaker: VIOLA
text: He must observe their mood on whom he jests,

speaker: VIOLA
text: The quality of persons, and the time,

speaker: VIOLA
text: And, like the haggard, check at every feather

speaker: VIOLA
text: That comes before his eye. This is a practice

speaker: VIOLA
text: As full of labor as a wise man’s art:

speaker: VIOLA
text: For folly that he wisely shows is fit;

speaker: VIOLA
text: But wise men, folly-fall’n, quite taint their wit.

speaker: TOBY
text: Save you, gentleman.

speaker: VIOLA
text: And you, sir.

speaker: ANDREW
text: Dieu vous garde, monsieur.

speaker: VIOLA
text: Et vous aussi. Votre serviteur!

speaker: ANDREW
text: I hope, sir, you are, and I am yours.

speaker: TOBY
text: Will you encounter the house? My niece is

speaker: TOBY
text: desirous you should enter, if your trade be to her.

speaker: VIOLA
text: I am bound to your niece, sir; I mean, she is the

speaker: VIOLA
text: list of my voyage.

speaker: TOBY
text: Taste your legs, sir; put them to motion.

speaker: VIOLA
text: My legs do better understand me, sir, than I

speaker: VIOLA
text: understand what you mean by bidding me taste my

speaker: VIOLA
text: legs.

speaker: TOBY
text: I mean, to go, sir, to enter.

speaker: VIOLA
text: I will answer you with gait and entrance—but

speaker: VIOLA
text: we are prevented.

speaker: VIOLA
text: Most excellent accomplished lady, the heavens rain

speaker: VIOLA
text: odors on you!

speaker: ANDREW
text: That youth’s a rare courtier. “Rain

speaker: ANDREW
text: odors,” well.

speaker: VIOLA
text: My matter hath no voice, lady, but to your own

speaker: VIOLA
text: most pregnant and vouchsafed ear.

speaker: ANDREW
text: “Odors,” “pregnant,” and “vouchsafed.”

speaker: ANDREW
text: I’ll get ’em all three all ready.

speaker: OLIVIA
text: Let the garden door be shut, and leave me to

speaker: OLIVIA
text: my hearing.

speaker: OLIVIA
text: Give me your hand, sir.

speaker: VIOLA
text: My duty, madam, and most humble service.

speaker: OLIVIA
text: What is your name?

speaker: VIOLA
text: Cesario is your servant’s name, fair princess.

speaker: OLIVIA
text: My servant, sir? ’Twas never merry world

speaker: OLIVIA
text: Since lowly feigning was called compliment.

speaker: OLIVIA
text: You’re servant to the Count Orsino, youth.

speaker: VIOLA
text: And he is yours, and his must needs be yours.

speaker: VIOLA
text: Your servant’s servant is your servant, madam.

speaker: OLIVIA
text: For him, I think not on him. For his thoughts,

speaker: OLIVIA
text: Would they were blanks rather than filled with me.

speaker: VIOLA
text: Madam, I come to whet your gentle thoughts

speaker: VIOLA
text: On his behalf.

speaker: OLIVIA
text: O, by your leave, I pray you.

speaker: OLIVIA
text: I bade you never speak again of him.

speaker: OLIVIA
text: But would you undertake another suit,

speaker: OLIVIA
text: I had rather hear you to solicit that

speaker: OLIVIA
text: Than music from the spheres.

speaker: VIOLA
text: Dear lady—

speaker: OLIVIA
text: Give me leave, beseech you. I did send,

speaker: OLIVIA
text: After the last enchantment you did here,

speaker: OLIVIA
text: A ring in chase of you. So did I abuse

speaker: OLIVIA
text: Myself, my servant, and, I fear me, you.

speaker: OLIVIA
text: Under your hard construction must I sit,

speaker: OLIVIA
text: To force that on you in a shameful cunning

speaker: OLIVIA
text: Which you knew none of yours. What might you think?

speaker: OLIVIA
text: Have you not set mine honor at the stake

speaker: OLIVIA
text: And baited it with all th’ unmuzzled thoughts

speaker: OLIVIA
text: That tyrannous heart can think? To one of your receiving

speaker: OLIVIA
text: Enough is shown. A cypress, not a bosom,

speaker: OLIVIA
text: Hides my heart. So, let me hear you speak.

speaker: VIOLA
text: I pity you.

speaker: OLIVIA
text: That’s a degree to love.

speaker: VIOLA
text: No, not a grize, for ’tis a vulgar proof

speaker: VIOLA
text: That very oft we pity enemies.

speaker: OLIVIA
text: Why then methinks ’tis time to smile again.

speaker: OLIVIA
text: O world, how apt the poor are to be proud!

speaker: OLIVIA
text: If one should be a prey, how much the better

speaker: OLIVIA
text: To fall before the lion than the wolf.

speaker: OLIVIA
text: The clock upbraids me with the waste of time.

speaker: OLIVIA
text: Be not afraid, good youth, I will not have you.

speaker: OLIVIA
text: And yet when wit and youth is come to harvest,

speaker: OLIVIA
text: Your wife is like to reap a proper man.

speaker: OLIVIA
text: There lies your way, due west.

speaker: VIOLA
text: Then westward ho!

speaker: VIOLA
text: Grace and good disposition attend your Ladyship.

speaker: VIOLA
text: You’ll nothing, madam, to my lord by me?

speaker: OLIVIA
text: Stay. I prithee, tell me what thou think’st of me.

speaker: VIOLA
text: That you do think you are not what you are.

speaker: OLIVIA
text: If I think so, I think the same of you.

speaker: VIOLA
text: Then think you right. I am not what I am.

speaker: OLIVIA
text: I would you were as I would have you be.

speaker: VIOLA
text: Would it be better, madam, than I am?

speaker: VIOLA
text: I wish it might, for now I am your fool.

speaker: OLIVIA
text: O, what a deal of scorn looks beautiful

speaker: OLIVIA
text: In the contempt and anger of his lip!

speaker: OLIVIA
text: A murd’rous guilt shows not itself more soon

speaker: OLIVIA
text: Than love that would seem hid. Love’s night is noon.—

speaker: OLIVIA
text: Cesario, by the roses of the spring,

speaker: OLIVIA
text: By maidhood, honor, truth, and everything,

speaker: OLIVIA
text: I love thee so, that, maugre all thy pride,

speaker: OLIVIA
text: Nor wit nor reason can my passion hide.

speaker: OLIVIA
text: Do not extort thy reasons from this clause,

speaker: OLIVIA
text: For that I woo, thou therefore hast no cause;

speaker: OLIVIA
text: But rather reason thus with reason fetter:

speaker: OLIVIA
text: Love sought is good, but given unsought is better.

speaker: VIOLA
text: By innocence I swear, and by my youth,

speaker: VIOLA
text: I have one heart, one bosom, and one truth,

speaker: VIOLA
text: And that no woman has, nor never none

speaker: VIOLA
text: Shall mistress be of it, save I alone.

speaker: VIOLA
text: And so adieu, good madam. Nevermore

speaker: VIOLA
text: Will I my master’s tears to you deplore.

speaker: OLIVIA
text: Yet come again, for thou perhaps mayst move

speaker: OLIVIA
text: That heart, which now abhors, to like his love.
